key_insight: An ML-enhanced multi-minima iterative genetic algorithm can discover novel crystal structures without relying on existing structural databases.
engineering_relevance: This approach improves computational crystal structure prediction for complex, chemically frustrated materials, helping guide synthesis of new magnetic and quantum phases.
method: Combines a genetic algorithm with an ANN-based interatomic potential
Uses an iterative, metadynamics-inspired penalty scheme to escape previously found minima
Explores both global ground states and nearby metastable competing structures
evidence_signal: The method correctly predicted the Pbam ground-state structure of La4Co4Pb and exactly matched the independently x-ray-determined orthorhombic structure of La5CoPb2 using only composition as input.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and transferability of the ANN potential and on sufficient sampling of a highly complex energy landscape.
paper_title: Complex crystal structure prediction using ML-enhanced multi-minima iterative genetic algorithm
